sanskrit: पश्य मे पार्थ रूपाणि शतशोऽथ सहस्रशः। नानाविधानि दिव्यानि नानावर्णाकृतीनि च।।
transliteration: paśya me pārtha rūpāṇi śataśo ’tha sahasraśaḥ nānā-vidhāni divyāni nānā-varṇākṛtīni ca
literal_meaning: Behold, O Pārtha, My forms—hundreds, then thousands—varied, divine, of manifold colors and shapes.
contextual_meaning: Krishna invites Arjuna to witness the boundless multiplicity of the Self, a panoramic exposure meant to shatter the narrow ego-container and evoke awe-based surrender.
psychological_state: Pre-awe tension: ego structures loosening before transpersonal expansion; liminal space between known identity and infinite possibility.
trigger_scenario: Standing before a vast night sky or VR simulation that reveals the scale of the universe, suddenly realizing one's story is one pixel in an infinite mosaic.
anchor_text: See every face as one radiant Self in countless disguises.
